content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA containing NNN positive integers. Find the number of pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that 1≤i<j≤N1 \leq i \lt j \leq N1≤i<j≤N and:
Ai+Aj≥Ai⋅AjA_i + A_j \geq A_i \cdot A_jAi​+Aj​≥Ai​⋅Aj​


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The TTT test cases then follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the required answer.
constraints: 2 ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N, the size of the array. The next line contains N space‑separated positive integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of pairs (i, j) (1 ≤ i < j ≤ N) such that Ai + Aj ≥ Ai · Aj.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: cnt1=1, cnt2=0 → pairs with a 1 = 1*(3-1)+0 = 2, no (2,2) pairs. Answer 2.
Test case 2: cnt1=0, cnt2=1 → no (2,2) pair, answer 0.
cnt1=0, cnt2=3 → C(3,2)=3 pairs of (2,2). No other valid pairs.

cnt1=2, cnt2=2, N=5.
Pairs with at least one 1: 2*(5-2)+C(2,2)=2*3+1=7.
Pairs of two 2's: C(2,2)=1.
Total = 7+1 = 8.

hidden.label: general case with no valid pairs

solution.approach:
For positive integers the inequality Ai + Aj ≥ Ai·Aj can be rewritten as (Ai‑1)*(Aj‑1) ≤ 1. This is only possible in three situations: • At least one of the numbers equals 1 (any partner works). • Both numbers equal 2. No other values satisfy the condition. Therefore we only need to count how many 1’s (cnt1) and how many 2’s (cnt2) appear in the array. The answer is:
    pairs with at least one 1 = cnt1 * (N - cnt1) + C(cnt1, 2)
    pairs of two 2's = C(cnt2, 2)
    total = cnt1 * (N - cnt1) + cnt1*(cnt1-1)/2 + cnt2*(cnt2-1)/2.
The algorithm scans the array once, counts cnt1 and cnt2, and computes the formula using 64‑bit integers.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (single pass). Overall O(sum of N) ≤ O(2·10^5).
